summary: Plan 10 is implementation-complete and independently re-reviewed after the protected-policy acceptance repair. Canonical tutorial state, all seven protected settings, every credited task definition, Guardrail receipts, and the shared five-case backend/frontend policy matrix are now exact and regression-covered through real direct and guided authoring. No correctness, binding, parity, evidence, or resume blocker remains.

review_basis.head: c66231eb06df5a67421ff3649c7d561fdf3ac250
review_basis.baseline: Fresh full-current review of the final uncommitted Plan 10 protected-policy repair in the isolated feat/aws-ecs-program worktree. The review covered canonical tutorial capture, seven-setting manifest inventory, all task-definition consumers, Guardrail receipt extraction and persistence, the shared five-case backend/frontend matrix, real direct/guided/YAML authoring, runtime and delayed export gates, and exact raw-fixture/live-backend projection equality.

warnings.detail: Filigree elspeth-6285c29c07 is in progress under the Plan 10 repair owner. The fresh full review authorizes re-close after the reviewed repair is committed on feat/aws-ecs-program.

Any diagnosed signed trust-tier/trust-boundary drift requires the operator-held review and signing path. The implementing agent diagnoses and reruns required-mode gates; it does not receive keys or self-sign allowlist repairs.

The local ECR acceptance images are temporary digest-pinned evidence artifacts and are always deleted before GO. Durable lean-image publication needs a separate trusted workflow for the GO SHA and approved platforms with verified SBOM, provenance, signature, and vulnerability policy; the current default-all GHCR/ACR workflow is not that path.

The disposable Terraform stacks, IAM policies, Cognito identity, ECR repository, browser runner, evidence destination, and resource-tag coverage are outside this repository. Missing operator inventory or permission is BLOCKED in Plan 12, never a local substitute for live acceptance.

resolved_findings.resolution: The impossible post-dependency RED extras step is now a passing frozen-lock precondition that returns ownership to Plans 02/06/07 on failure.
INSTALL_EXTRAS is non-empty, glob-disabled, token-validated, positional-argument based, mirrored across both sync layers, and exercised by invalid as well as default/lean Docker builds.

The Dockerfile's stale health guidance is repaired; the runbook uses parseable JSON for a proxy-independent exact-200 Python loopback health command and mechanically checks start period, service grace, Fargate 1.4, platform mapping, and task architecture.

The acceptance HTTP boundary now has one exact origin, explicit auth-mode exclusivity, no redirects, same-origin enforcement, concrete time/size budgets, strict atomic state handling, and adversarial tests.

Every intentional service launch/rollback forces and proves a new deployment identity; Scenario B initializes fresh Aurora with the rollback image before baseline web startup, then runs the candidate doctor before mutation.

Root-running ECS Exec is no longer credited for EFS or SQLite proof. Payload and local-auth verification use explicit-UID one-shot task definitions, with local auth drained to fixed 503 and desired-zero before opening auth.db read-only.

The controller-side real-S3 suite is supplemented by per-task ECS task-role/default-chain S3 source/sink/hash/collision/cleanup proof with exact test-only prefix-scoped permissions and environment names.

The OIDC harness is single-spec, single-worker, artifact-disabled, custom-redacted, bounded, atomic-evidence writing, and credential-free under --list; Plan 12 proves Chromium before external mutation.

EventBridge validation now checks the CloudWatch Logs resource policy and no RoleArn, then proves delivery with a temporary custom-source canary rule targeting the same destination and removes it before deployment.

All AWS CLI calls are routed through the bounded stdout/stderr capture wrapper and closed evidence projector; raw logs, provider errors, task definitions, and AWS stderr are never retained as acceptance evidence.
Task 8 always deletes temporary image tags, confirms the original acceptance-run tag/service orphan sweep before clearing CLEANUP_REQUIRED, and cannot promote an image before final GO.
The fixed YAML is parsed and runtime-validated against the live importer with explicit source binding, sink policy, schema, and allowlisted unique output path instead of relying on HTTP mocks.

The runbook has Bash syntax and safety-contract regression coverage; Plan 10 adds manifest, inventory, static, signed-trust, Wardline, frontend, staged-hook, and affected-route closeout gates without --no-verify.
Plan 10 and Plan 12 Filigree fields now name the real file set, force-new/baseline/non-root/task-role/platform/evidence/cleanup obligations, while both issues remain unclaimed and untransitioned.

The full contract repair makes initial evidence-export replay historical and idempotent, binds exact positive telemetry query/sentinel/namespace/trace identities through immutable strict-superset checkpoints in both Plan 12 and the canonical runbook, enforces paired retained counts, seals cleanup attempts, and resumes safely when task8.check05 is written before the manifest commit.

The acceptance controller and runbook bind every candidate, doctor, verifier, rollback, and previous task definition byte-for-byte to the protected scenario inventory for seven settings, policy binding, live model, and region; Guardrail receipts are checked again when extracted and persisted.

A single five-case fixture now proves exact live backend and frontend policy projections plus real direct, guided, YAML import, validation, execution, and delayed-export outcomes; public operator-profile aliases lower through the production catalog path.

verification: Loomweave incremental refresh completed fresh at current HEAD after the composer YAML commit

Dockerfile, .dockerignore, pyproject extras/lock ownership, build-push workflow, release checks, REST routes, settings loader, LandscapeDB, payload store, and YAML importer reconciled against live source
official AWS ECS deployment, runtimePlatform, health-check, ECS Exec, task-role, and ECR scanning documentation reconciled
Plans 00, 06, 07, 09, 10, 12, 13 and the runtime-readiness spec reconciled
POSIX INSTALL_EXTRAS validation executed against valid, empty, whitespace, leading-option, uppercase, glob, and metacharacter inputs
task-definition health command parsed as JSON
Plan 10 and Plan 12 tracker fields updated through typed Filigree MCP without claim or status transition

Current Plan 10 differs from the reviewed PROGRAM_BASE_SHA only by the completed Plan 15B policy/schema handoff and Plan 15C reusable Guardrail proof handoff; the exact delta was re-reviewed with no new blocker
All 18 preceding milestone items have valid feat/aws-ecs-program close anchors that are ancestors of af4aece2c90285264f887f3646e48f12ed27227e
three independent specialist re-reviews plus current-HEAD synthesis reported zero remaining blockers
fresh full-current Plan 10 review returned GO after 257 focused tests, Ruff check and format, mypy, runbook and Plan 12 Bash syntax checks, and git diff --check
final frozen-tree review returned GO after exact raw-fixture/live-backend projection equality closed the final substitution counterexample
4654 backend tests and 2324 frontend tests pass; Ruff check, Ruff format-check, mypy, and git diff --check are clean

review_history.summary: Initial review found unsafe extras parsing, stale/non-executable health wiring, unbound architecture, unforced desired-zero launches, missing rollback-baseline schema initialization, root-masked EFS and concurrent SQLite verification, no ECS task-role S3 proof, raw evidence paths, incomplete OIDC isolation, absent EventBridge delivery proof, premature image promotion, and state-empty-only cleanup.

Re-reviewed the exact post-review Plan 15B and 15C handoff delta and all 18 live close anchors. The additions correctly preserve implemented policy/schema compatibility and reuse the completed Guardrail checker without changing task order, rollback-baseline semantics, or Plan 12 ownership.

Fresh full-plan review found and repaired canonical-runbook telemetry drift, non-exact retained queries, mismatched metric/trace counts, historical export replay failure, cleanup ledger phase hazards, and the terminal-row-before-manifest-commit crash window. Independent re-review returned GO with 257 focused tests and all static/syntax checks clean.

Final frozen-tree re-review returned GO. Canonical tutorial semantics, exact seven-setting task-definition and receipt trust anchors, full five-case backend/frontend parity, and real authoring paths are closed with 4654 backend and 2324 frontend tests passing.
